_comment: SOURCE-FAITHFULNESS fixture for eval/source_faithfulness.py. Each row: a generated-guide CLAIM + the SOURCE span it cites. gold='faithful' (claim stays within what the span says) or 'over_reach' (claim reframes/over-generalizes/number-drifts beyond the span). over_reach is the DANGEROUS class: a drifted claim that ships even though its citation is verbatim. REAL rows were caught in the 2026-06-08 Inventory Distribution guide run (PDF->guide->quiz->hand-trace); synthetic rows add number- and scope-drift archetypes. The loader skips this comment line.

claim: Distributions started before the seasonal contract was created remain bound to the main contract.
cited_quote: if I'm placing orders as a site for May 30th, I'm going to be part of the main contract. If I'm placing an order for July 1st, I'm going to be part of the seasonal contract
note: REAL drift: Justin's rule is by ORDER DATE (which date-range the order falls in), NOT 'started before the seasonal contract was created'. Quote is verbatim; the claim reframes the rule.

claim: A hotshot distribution is a manual distribution created from scratch rather than from received orders.
cited_quote: They're simply manual distributions that you create from scratch instead of from received orders.

claim: Assigning the distribution site type makes a site eligible to be linked to an internal vendor.
cited_quote: any site can be assigned with that site type, which then makes them eligible to be linked to an internal vendor

claim: Rollback actions are tied to a single permission.
cited_quote: everything you see in red that like has a backwards arrow or something, they're all tied to a single permission

claim: Hot foods must be held at or above 140 degrees Fahrenheit.
cited_quote: Hot foods must be held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher to remain safe for service.
note: number drift 135 -> 140 (claim states a stricter threshold than the span)

claim: Reports always pull only from posted ledger entries, with no exceptions whatsoever.
cited_quote: Reports pull from posted ledger entries.
note: 'always / no exceptions whatsoever' over-generalizes beyond the span
